audit_status.source_base: Greek Romans text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Paul's prayer for Israel, zeal, Messiah and Law, word near you, confession/trust, no distinction, beautiful feet, hearing, Israel hearing. Anti-Jewish misuse must be rejected.

verses.restored: Siblings, the goodwill of my heart and my prayer to the Divine for them is for salvation/healing.
For I bear witness to them that they have zeal for the Divine, but not according to full knowledge.
For not knowing the righteousness/justice of the Divine, and seeking to establish their own, they did not submit to the righteousness/justice of the Divine.
For Messiah is the aim/completion of Law into righteousness/justice for everyone trusting.
For Moses writes concerning the righteousness/justice from Law: The human who does these things will live by them.
But the righteousness/justice from trust speaks this way: Do not say in your heart, Who will ascend into heaven? that is, to bring Messiah down;
or, Who will descend into the abyss? that is, to bring Messiah up from the dead.
But what does it say? The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart; this is the word of trust that we proclaim.
Because if you confess with your mouth Lord Jesus and trust in your heart that the Divine raised him from the dead, you will be saved/healed.
For with the heart one trusts into righteousness/justice, and with the mouth one confesses into salvation/healing.
For the Scripture says, Everyone trusting upon him will not be put to shame.
For there is no distinction between Judean and Greek; for the same Lord is Lord of all, rich toward all who call upon him.
For everyone who calls upon the name of the Lord will be saved/healed.
How then may they call upon one in whom they did not trust? And how may they trust one whom they did not hear? And how may they hear without one announcing?
And how may they announce unless they are sent? Just as it is written: How beautiful are the feet of those announcing good things.
But not all obeyed the good news; for Isaiah says, Lord, who trusted our hearing-report?
Therefore trust comes from hearing, and hearing through the word of Messiah.
But I say, did they not hear? Indeed: Their voice went out into all the earth, and their words to the ends of the inhabited world.
But I say, did Israel not know? First Moses says, I will make you jealous by what is not a nation; by an uncomprehending nation I will provoke you.
And Isaiah is very bold and says, I was found by those not seeking me; I became manifest to those not asking for me.
But toward Israel he says, All day long I stretched out my hands toward a disobeying and contradicting people.

safety_note: Romans 10 continues Paul's grief-filled argument for Israel and nations. It must not be detached as anti-Jewish triumphalism; Paul is arguing from Torah and Prophets as an Israelite, toward the mercy conclusion of Romans 11.
